title: carex latebracteata
intro: Carex latebracteata, also known as late-bract sedge, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. Carex latebracteata can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
